name: Copper CRM API
what_it_does: Copper CRM REST API — programmatic access to contacts, leads, opportunities, and activities in the Google Workspace-native CRM, enabling agents to automate sales workflows deeply integrated with Gmail and Google Calendar.
use_cases: Agents automatically creating leads from Gmail conversations and syncing email activity to Copper CRM
Pipeline automation — agents updating opportunity stages based on email sentiment analysis and deal signals
Contact enrichment — agents adding company and social data to Copper contact records from external enrichment APIs
Activity logging — agents recording meeting notes and call summaries directly to CRM contact timelines
Google Calendar sync — agents scheduling follow-up tasks in Copper tied to Google Calendar events
not_for: Non-Google Workspace teams — Copper's differentiation is Google integration; use HubSpot for platform-agnostic CRM
Complex enterprise CRM needs — Copper is optimized for SMBs; use Salesforce for enterprise-scale workflows
High-volume automated prospecting — Copper lacks native sequences; use Apollo or Outreach for sales automation
best_when: Your team lives in Google Workspace and needs a CRM that agents can automate while maintaining Gmail/Calendar as the primary interface.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use Google Workspace — Copper's value proposition disappears without the Google integration.

auth.notes: API key + user email required in request headers (X-PW-AccessToken and X-PW-UserEmail). API key scoped to a specific user's permissions. Actions performed as that user in CRM. No OAuth for third-party apps.

pricing.notes: API access included in all plans. 14-day free trial available. No free tier. Annual billing discounts available.

performance.rate_limits: 600 requests/minute per API key; bulk operations limited; webhooks near-real-time

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with reasonable documentation. Error responses include codes and messages. Custom field handling requires extra API calls to resolve field IDs. Webhook payloads are well-structured.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Contact/lead creation is not idempotent — duplicates can be created. Search-before-create pattern recommended. Updates by ID are idempotent. Activity creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Both API key AND user email required in every request header — missing either returns 401
Custom fields require separate API call to fetch field definitions before reading/writing custom field values
Copper uses entity-specific search (not global) — separate search endpoints for leads, people, companies, opportunities
Rate limit (600 req/min) shared across all API keys — in multi-agent scenarios coordinate to avoid throttling
Relationship linking (contact to company) requires separate PATCH requests after entity creation

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant. API key with user email — unusual auth pattern. No scope granularity — key has same permissions as the user. US data processing. Audit logs available in Business plan.
